title: THE SURVIVOR — ECHO DIRECTOR'S CUT V5
episodeObjective: Lock channel 7.14, establish Maya as a living survivor, guide her through lower access spine co-action choices, manage Hale/Maya conflict, recover or sacrifice evidence, and end with a consequence that directly shapes Episode 05.
directionRules: 1. Every episode has a concrete objective.
2. Characters stay physically active and move through real spaces.
3. The player directs character actions; the player is the operator, not a spectator.
4. Every decision has a visible consequence.
5. Some consequences are irreversible.
6. Every visible choice group has exactly two choices.
7. Choices create real branch consequences, not cosmetic routes.
8. Characters behave like real people.
9. Characters can psychologically change.
10. Characters can react negatively to the player.
11. Characters can react negatively to each other.
12. Characters remember previous decisions.
13. Every character has a distinct voice.
14. Characters can grow or break.
15. No meaningless dialogue.
16. Report-style action language is forbidden where lived action can replace it.
17. Characters experience actions instead of summarizing completed actions.
18. Character emotion must be visible in dialogue.
19. Characters are imperfect: hesitation, mistakes, interruptions, reversals.
20. Character first, information second.
21. Lore is delivered through lived character experience.
22. Every scene contains a micro-human moment.
23. Horror comes from absence, corrupted memory, and contradictions.
24. UNKNOWN must push story forward, not just glitch.
25. Trust values must become visible in later dialogue.
26. Pressured characters change behavior.
27. Irreversible loss must actually remove or damage something.
28. CharacterBusy is used only for important physical actions.
29. Every episode ends with a new major question.
30. Every episode has at least one consequence that makes the player feel responsible.

frequency_714.title: SIGNAL TUNING
frequency_714.description: Tune the emergency frequency until Maya's unstable survivor transmission locks.

e04_n01.speaker: SYSTEM
e04_n01.objective: Open channel 7.14 and catch the first unstable survivor transmission.

e04_n02_first_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Rude breathing. I will take that.
I've been talking to static too long.
The relay casing is sliding under my hand.

e04_n02_first_voice.objective: The player chooses whether to connect emotionally first or treat Maya as an unknown signal.

choices.text: TELL HER YOU CAN HEAR HER.

e04_n03_identify.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Keep me on the channel and I will give you my name.

e04_n03_identify.objective: Maya reacts better to direct human contact but still needs technical help.

e04_n03_verify.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Anything that answers too fast is usually lying.

e04_n03_verify.objective: Maya is irritated by verification, but respects the discipline.

e04_n03_trace.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.title: EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT
events.content:
CHANNEL: 7.14 MHz
SIGNAL STATE: DISTORTED
VOICEPRINT: UNKNOWN FEMALE
RECOVERED FRAGMENTS:

"...anyone..."
"...relay is drifting..."
"...not Hale..."
"...lower access spine..."

Recommended action: manual signal tuning required.
events.caption: Text reconstruction of distorted emergency audio from channel 7.14.

events.text: Relay casing is kicking under my palm.
Every time it kicks, the line slides.

e04_n03_trace.objective: Player decides whether to trust the new signal or consult Hale first.

e04_n04_hale_warning.speaker: HALE

events.text: And if she gives you a name...
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: don't let the name do the thinking.
events.speaker: HALE

e04_n04_hale_warning.objective: Hale warns the operator without fully stopping the rescue.

e04_n04_tune.speaker: SYSTEM
e04_n04_tune.objective: Tune 7.14 without letting the carrier drift out of reach.

e04_n04_fail.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: I had you for a second. Don't make me go back to static.
Hold it steady when it bites. The drift gets worse right before the lock.

e04_n04_fail.objective: Failure no longer stalls; the player can retry or review a clue.

e04_n04_hint.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Listen. Not to the words. To the pull.
It drifts below seven, then snaps back. It wants you to overcorrect.
Don't chase it. Let it come to 7.14 and hold there.
I hate that I sound like Hale right now.

e04_n04_hint.objective: Offer a clue while preserving the two-choice rule.

choices.text: ASK HALE FOR A STABILIZATION TIP.

e04_n04_hale_tip.speaker: HALE

events.text: Let it drift into your hand.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: And don't tell Maya I helped.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: She will survive just to be smug.
events.speaker: HALE

e04_n04_hale_tip.objective: Hale contributes operationally, but involving him affects Maya.

e04_n05_signal_locked.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I forgot how to talk to someone who answers back.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I was starting to think I'd die insulting static.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If Ethan Hale is on your other channel...
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n05_signal_locked.objective: Maya becomes a living person with a physical location and urgent survival objective.

e04_n06_maya_position.speaker: MAYA

events.title: SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN
events.content:
CHANNEL SOURCE: 7.14 MHz
VOICEPRINT MATCH: MAYA CHEN
LIFE SIGNS: ACTIVE
OXYGEN: 18%
LOCATION ESTIMATE: DAMAGED RELAY CONSOLE / LOWER ACCESS SPINE
VISUAL FEED: UNAVAILABLE
STATUS: ALIVE / CRITICAL
events.caption: Automated survivor verification generated from channel telemetry. No image recovered.

events.text: My lungs are calling it a liar.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Console's holding the strut off my leg.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You name things when you're alone.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n06_maya_position.objective: First true operation decision: data vs survival pace.

choices.text: MOVE MAYA TOWARD THE ACCESS SPINE.

e04_n07_console_route.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Malcolm and I are about to have a workplace dispute.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Local buffer is blinking behind the glass.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I need to shift the strut without dropping it.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If I go quiet, both hands are busy.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n07_console_route.objective: Staying gives a reward but reveals danger.

e04_n07_move_route.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm dragging the strut off slow.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If I swear, blame the channel.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n07_move_route.objective: Moving preserves oxygen but loses early telemetry.

e04_n08_hale_interrupt.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Of course that's the first thing he says.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You don't get to use my name like a brake.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I'm at a pressure lock with bad air and one good leg.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I want the next door open.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n08_hale_interrupt.objective: Player manages interpersonal conflict while Maya is physically vulnerable.

e04_n09_mute_hale.speaker: MAYA

events.text: I know that probably felt wrong.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He makes silence feel like betrayal.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Now get me through Service Lock C before whatever is using his signal notices I'm moving.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n09_mute_hale.objective: Maya opens up more if Hale is muted, but Hale may remember this later.

e04_n09_both_channels.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But if he steers this, I cut the channel myself.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Technically you would interrupt the carrier.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Operator, both channels are fighting the carrier.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Then help me open a door instead of narrating the funeral.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n09_both_channels.objective: Keeping both channels preserves Hale but increases signal stress.

e04_n10_route_choice.speaker: MAYA
e04_n10_route_choice.objective: Second operation decision: safe/slow vs fast/dangerous.

e04_n11_crawlway.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If I drop out, it's because I'm inside a metal throat.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n11_crawlway.objective: Safer path preserves stealth but plants horror.

e04_n11_main_spine.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If I say run, assume I started early.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n11_main_spine.objective: Faster path saves oxygen but raises Maya's stress and unknown attention.

e04_n12_crew_memory.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Like it can't decide whether to save me or make a point.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Six of us were assigned to ECHO.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Hale. Rourke. Lin. Patel. Arman. Me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Patel fixed coffee ratios when nobody watched.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Arman sang when he was nervous.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: He sang a lot near the end.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n12_crew_memory.objective: Conversation happens during movement, not instead of movement.

choices.text: WHAT DO YOU NEED RIGHT NOW?

e04_n13_proof.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But the station is good at handing out records after witnesses are gone.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Not the sound Hale made when the corridor decompressed.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Get me to the terminal and I'll pull my statement.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n13_proof.objective: Proof path increases tension but gives a concrete next action.

e04_n13_need.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Then a room where nothing knows my name.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Operationally: Service Lock C opens without cycling the outer seal.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Emotionally: ask me when my lungs stop voting against me.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n13_need.objective: Survival-focused path strengthens Maya as a person and a rescue target.

e04_n14_terminal.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Terminal is a few meters away.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n14_terminal.objective: End on a rescue decision that affects Maya's emotional state going into Episode 05.

e04_r07_console_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r07_console_return.objective: Maya returns after extracting telemetry from the damaged relay console.

events.text: Buffer kept dropping when I shifted my weight.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: MAYA RELAY TELEMETRY
events.content:
LOWER ACCESS SPINE
OXYGEN BLEED: ACTIVE
AIRFLOW SOURCE: SERVICE LOCK C
SECONDARY MOVEMENT TRACE: DETECTED NEAR DOOR 12 RETURN LINE
NOTE: trace does not match Maya's current position.
events.caption: Maya extracted this while stabilizing the damaged relay console.

events.text: Also yes. Something else is moving.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_r07_move_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r07_move_return.objective: Maya returns after leaving the damaged console.

events.text: Smells like hot plastic and old blood.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: But I'm farther from the leak.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_r11_crawlway_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r11_crawlway_return.objective: Maya returns after clearing the crawlway.

events.text: I'm putting that in a box marked later.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_r11_main_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r11_main_return.objective: Maya returns after crossing the main access spine.

events.text: Something moved behind me halfway across.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Hit my shoulder on the frame.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Said a word that would get me banned from two briefings.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_r14_terminal_return.speaker: MAYA
e04_r14_terminal_return.objective: Maya returns from the Service Lock C terminal with her survivor statement.

events.text: Choosing not to identify the substance.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: SURVIVOR STATEMENT: MAYA
events.content:
TEXT TRANSCRIPT / CHANNEL 7.14

MAYA CHEN:
"There were six assigned crew members on ECHO. After the breach, only one confirmed biological survivor remained: me. The signal using Ethan Hale's identity is not a living crew member. Do not allow it to control station routing."

Transcript confidence: 91%
events.caption: Text transcript reconstructed from Maya's stabilized emergency channel.

events.text: Like the station is politely avoiding the word alone.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Do not let it turn this into a trial.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n15_press_memory.speaker: MAYA
e04_n15_press_memory.objective: Press Maya for memory and create a trust wound that carries into Episode 05.

events.text: But his voice was already on the comm before the door opened.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Next time, ask before you put your hands in my head.
events.speaker: MAYA

e04_n15_seal_statement.speaker: MAYA
e04_n15_seal_statement.objective: Protect Maya's trust but delay the memory clue into Episode 05.

events.text: I know curiosity is screaming right now.
events.speaker: MAYA

upgradeFocus: Removed remaining report-style lines and replaced them with lived action beats.
Made Maya/Hale trust consequences visible inside branch dialogue.
Added irreversible tradeoffs: console telemetry can be recovered or lost, and Maya memory can be pressured or protected.
Strengthened UNKNOWN so it gives story information, not only atmosphere.
Reduced busy feeling by framing each busy event with active physical stakes.
Added final consequence nodes that lead into Episode 05 with different start flags.

summary: Progress bars replaced where appropriate with characterBusy + returnNodeId.
Maya speaks like a real exhausted survivor, not a lore dispenser.
Hale/Maya conflict is shown through behavior and friction, not UI trust alerts.
Every visible choice group has exactly two choices.
Physical actions and movement produce return reports.
No visible wait timer is exposed to the player.
Lore is delivered through lived experience, environment, and recovered evidence.

lifelineV6Pass.name: LIFELINE CINEMATIC PASS V2 / Episode 04 V6
focus: Characters speak while moving, crawling, opening locks, losing breath, and reacting to the immediate space.
Choice setup lines avoid explaining two options mechanically; the scene itself creates the decision.
Maya makes mistakes, jokes under stress, gets irritated, misunderstands the Operator, and recovers.
Hale and Maya conflict through personality, not UI trust text.
Long busy blocks are shortened and framed by live micro-messages so the player feels ongoing rescue momentum.

majorConsequences: Leaving the console preserves oxygen but permanently loses the early movement telemetry.
Stabilizing the console recovers Door 12 movement telemetry but costs oxygen.
Muting Hale gives Maya trust but creates a Hale resentment flag for Episode 05.
Keeping both channels preserves Hale trust but increases carrier instability.
Pressing Maya's memory increases UNKNOWN attention and damages Maya trust.
Sealing Maya's statement protects her trust but delays a memory clue into Episode 05.

episode05UsageNotes.irreversibleTelemetryLoss: The early movement trace near Door 12 is unavailable; Maya knows the operator chose air over data.
episode05UsageNotes.mayaTelemetryRecovered: The operator has proof of movement near the Door 12 return line, but Maya paid oxygen for it.
episode05UsageNotes.haleMutedEpisode04: Hale should remember being muted and begin colder or more wounded in Episode 05.
episode05UsageNotes.dualChannelStress: Maya should be less trusting if the operator kept Hale on during her rescue.
episode05UsageNotes.pressedMayaMemory: Maya enters Episode 05 guarded and angry; UNKNOWN has more hooks into her memory.
episode05UsageNotes.statementSealed: Maya trusts the operator more, but the Door 12 memory clue is delayed.
episode05UsageNotes.unknownAttention: If the main spine or memory pressure path was taken, UNKNOWN can become more direct.
